name: bitcorn-lightning-application
description: Root-level dev orchestration for the Bitcorn Lightning app. The app itself lives in app/api, app/web, and cloudflare-worker — see docs/dev-setup.md for how to run all three locally against a Polar regtest network.

scripts.dev:treasury: cd app/api && dotenv -e ../../.env.dev.treasury -- npm run dev
scripts.dev:memberA: cd app/api && dotenv -e ../../.env.dev.member-a  -- npm run dev
scripts.dev:memberB: cd app/api && dotenv -e ../../.env.dev.member-b  -- npm run dev
scripts.dev:webT: cd app/web && VITE_API_BASE=http://localhost:3101 npm run dev -- --port 5173 --strictPort
scripts.dev:webA: cd app/web && VITE_API_BASE=http://localhost:3102 npm run dev -- --port 5174 --strictPort
scripts.dev:webB: cd app/web && VITE_API_BASE=http://localhost:3103 npm run dev -- --port 5175 --strictPort
scripts.dev:kill: lsof -ti:3101,3102,3103,5173,5174,5175,11009,11010,11020,11030 | xargs -r kill -9 2>/dev/null; echo 'dev ports cleared (incl loop layer)'
scripts._comment_loop_layer: Optional Loop fidelity layer scripts. loopserver runs as a Docker container (Lightning Labs publishes lightninglabs/loopserver:latest — there is no Go source for it in lightninglabs/loop). loopd runs as a native binary built from lightninglabs/loop v0.33.0-beta. See docs/dev-setup.md § Optional: Loop Fidelity Layer for setup details.
scripts.dev:loopserver: docker rm -f bitcorn-loopserver 2>/dev/null; docker run -d --name bitcorn-loopserver --network host -v /home/user/.polar/networks/1/volumes/lnd/External-Peer-1:/root/.lnd:ro lightninglabs/loopserver:latest daemon --maxamt=5000000 --lnd.host=127.0.0.1:10007 --lnd.macaroondir=/root/.lnd/data/chain/bitcoin/regtest --lnd.tlspath=/root/.lnd/tls.cert --bitcoin.host=127.0.0.1:18444 --bitcoin.user=polaruser --bitcoin.password=polarpass --bitcoin.zmqpubrawblock=tcp://127.0.0.1:28335 --bitcoin.zmqpubrawtx=tcp://127.0.0.1:29336 && echo 'loopserver started on :11009 — check `docker logs bitcorn-loopserver` (persists on crash)'
